attacking_style: Opportunistic sprinter who launches from 200-250m out in disorganized finales, capitalizing when traditional sprint trains break down or fail to materialize.
team_tactics: Typically functions as leadout rider for designated sprinters; when leadout duties dissolve or sprinter disappears, shifts to personal sprint mode rather than following rigid team structure.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where chaos in the finale creates opportunities; thrives when organizational structure collapses and raw positioning ability matters more than pure power.
vulnerability: Well-organized sprint trains likely suppress his chances; designated team role as leadout man typically prevents him from contesting finishes when structure holds.
key_patterns: Wins from disorganized finales when sprint trains fail, exploiting positioning over power (2 instances)
Launches longer sprints (250m) compared to traditional leadout-delivered sprinters seeking slipstream advantage
Converts from leadout role to personal finish when team structure dissolves (2 instances)
Career success at age 35 suggests late-blooming tactical intelligence over declining physical capacity
form_trajectory: positive: opportunistically converted suppressed finishing speed into Grand Tour wins when team structure allowed personal ambition
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 7
